title: Fix deleted documents resurfacing after DO restart
description: The deletedDocs map is cleared on flush, but deletion markers aren't persisted in Parquet. After DO restart, deleted documents may resurface from R2 blocks. Need to persist deletion markers (tombstones) in Parquet files.

description: Critical testing gap: Zero E2E tests exist. Need tests that connect real MongoDB drivers (Node.js driver, mongosh) to MongoLake Worker. Should cover: connection, CRUD operations, aggregation, error handling. Run against deployed or local miniflare instance.

title: Import missing Cloudflare Workers types

title: Fix manifest durability gap causing orphaned R2 blocks
description: If R2 flush succeeds but manifest update fails, blocks become orphaned in R2 with potential data loss. Solution: Persist manifest to R2 as {collection}/_manifest.json atomically with block writes, or use two-phase commit.

title: Create performance benchmark test suite
description: 0 performance tests exist. Need benchmarks for: filter matching throughput, Parquet serialization speed, update operator performance, memory under load, query latency. Prevents unknown regressions.
notes: Reference existing benchmarks from parquedb: tests/benchmarks/ has 14 benchmark files including crud.bench.ts, queries.bench.ts, parquet.bench.ts, scalability.bench.ts, concurrency.bench.ts. Use setup.ts benchmark infrastructure with Timer utilities, BenchmarkStats (mean/median/p95/p99), warmup iterations. Also see examples/ for real datasets (ONET, IMDB, UNSPSC). Copy patterns from pocs/iceberg/iceberg-parquet-benchmark/ and pocs/indexing/hot-tier-index-poc/src/benchmarks/ for additional reference.

title: Epic: Schema Evolution & Field Promotion
description:
Implement configurable schema with field promotion to native Parquet columns.

Features:
- Configure columns per collection
- Auto-promote fields above threshold
- Native types: string, int, float, timestamp, struct, list
- Keep variant for full document
- Schema versioning
- Backward compatibility

title: Epic: Parquet Reader with Deduplication
description:

Key features:
- Read Parquet footer for row group metadata

- Deduplicate by _id, keep highest _seq
- Filter out deletes (_op='d')
- HTTP Range requests for surgical reads
- Support for native columns + variant fallback

title: Epic: Database Branching & Merging
description:
Implement Git-like branching and merging for databases.

Features:
- db.branch('feature-x') - create branch
- db.merge('feature-x') - merge branch
- db.diff('feature-x') - show changes
- Branch isolation (copy-on-write)
- Conflict detection and resolution
- Push/pull between local and remote

title: Epic: Streaming Parquet Writer
description:
Implement streaming Parquet writer that can write 500MB+ files with 128MB Worker memory using R2 multipart uploads. Core innovation enabling lakehouse storage.

Key features:
- Row group batching (~64MB per row group)
- R2 multipart upload integration
- Variant encoding for full documents
- Native column promotion for configured fields
- Zone maps and statistics per row group
- Footer written last with all metadata

Epic: MongoDB Wire Protocol Server
description:
Implement MongoDB wire protocol server for mongosh/Compass/driver compatibility.

Key features:
- TCP server (Bun.listen)
- OP_MSG parsing (MongoDB 3.6+)
- Command translation to MongoLake API
- Response encoding
- Connection management
- Authentication support

title: Message parser - parse MongoDB wire protocol messages
description: Parse MongoDB wire protocol messages including OP_MSG format and message headers. Handle the binary wire format with proper length prefixes, request IDs, and opcodes.

title: REFACTOR: Clean up message parser implementation
description: Refactor message parser: extract constants for opcodes, create typed interfaces for parsed messages, optimize buffer handling, add streaming parser support for large messages.

title: GREEN: Implement command decoder to pass tests

description: Refactor command decoder: create command type discriminated union, add validation for required fields, create builder pattern for complex commands, add support for additional commands (count, distinct, getMore).
